Roberto, we are halfway through. Let us review your progress. What is going well for you?
My biggest strength now is handling objections. I feel much more confident when a client says the price is too high.
That is excellent. And what would you like to improve?
The passive voice. Under pressure I forget it. My goal is to be more fluent and to overcome that.
Good. Here is my feedback: practice out loud every day. If you do that, you will reach the next milestone easily.
Thank you, Amy. I am proud of my progress, and I am ready for the second half.
I have made a lot of progress.
My biggest strength is handling objections.
I want to improve my fluency.
My goal is to speak more confidently.
Thank you for the feedback.

Hi Roberto, this is Amy. We are halfway through the program now, so this is a good moment to review your progress. In the last four lessons you learned a lot. In lesson eleven you ran a meeting and set an agenda. In lesson twelve you handled a negotiation and used the first conditional: if we order more, you will give us a discount. In lesson thirteen you presented the product in the passive voice: the engines are made in Resende, every unit is inspected. And in lesson fourteen you handled objections politely with should and could. I am really impressed. Your confidence has grown and you are much more fluent on calls. Today, think about your strengths and the things you still want to improve. Set one clear goal for the second half of the program. Talk to your teacher about your progress, and be honest about what is still challenging. This is your checkpoint. Let us see how far you have come.

This is my mid-program review. I want to be honest about my progress. Over the last four lessons, I have improved a lot. My biggest strength now is handling objections. When a client says the price is too high, I do not panic anymore. I make a polite suggestion and we reach a compromise. Running a meeting is also easier for me now. The thing I still need to improve is the passive voice. Sometimes I forget it under pressure on a real call, so that is my main goal for the next lessons. I also want to become more fluent, with fewer pauses. My teacher gave me good feedback: I should keep practicing out loud every day. I feel more confident than before. I am ready to overcome the next challenges and reach the milestone of lesson twenty.

First, look back at what you learned in the last four lessons. Then, name your biggest strength with a clear example. After that, choose one skill you still want to improve. Next, set one specific goal for the second half of the program. Finally, ask your teacher for feedback on how to reach it.
